item.title: Re: RE:
item.content:encoded: Just spotted the new revisions. The narrative flow is much, much more cohesive now. Some of the smaller grammar tweaks and formatting fixes really make this easier to read through. My +0 is now a +1. Good work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was fairly certain I knew what the ending and main storyline would be after reading 'heightened levels of self-respect and confidence' and became absolutely certain of it after the third test. Unfortunately, that also means I got absolutely nothing out of this, because it follows the exact same path trodden by SCP-2790, SCP-4511, and SCP-1561, among others — except with far less interesting results and an utterly inconsequential ending.

item.title: Re: SCP-4765
item.content:encoded: I remember first seeing this in the forums with no comments. You’ve improved it a lot since then. Great job! I really love that you could do the SCP affecting the logs in a proper way, many fail at it. Great job!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I guess, after two similar comments, I will have a look at the first addendum. Maybe the additional subtlety would work, however it didn’t seem to in the draft phase.

However, it will be some time before I can make major changes

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really enjoy the slow descent into madness you seem to display, but I will agree with a comment above about subtlety. I think there is a need for a few adjustments to the pacing or wording in order for the effects of the item itself to shine. Overall though I still love the concept and the article, good work.

+1 from me, and after a bit of adjusting I would never doubt myself on it.

item.title: RE:
item.content:encoded:
You’re right about an image, however I cannot find one with the acceptable licensing.

I have added the title of Head Researcher to Dr Dedrick

The test log is now in a collapsible too

For the addendum, you are correct that it seems a bit early. However, people struggled to understand the anomaly without it, so I feel it is necessary.

I am working on improving the journal text. Thanks a bunch for the formatting advice though!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good work on this gee0765. I'm tempted to +1, but there are some issues that could use a revision.

Crit and comments
Crit and comments

1. It could use an image. The wikicommons and copyright free searches of google didn't have any images of mirrors that basic in construction?

2. Dr. Philip Dedrick needs an official title. Director, Head Researcher, Project Director, etc. There's plenty of Doctors within the foundation. Something that shows his authority to make these revisions. "Doctor Santos, the new Head Researcher" implies Dr. Philip Dedrick should be noted as Head Researcher first.

3. The sections of your scip, say between the description and test logs, aren't especially well divided. Page breaks, collapsibles, and just a clearer cut-off in narrative is required.

4. "I’m fucking sick of this elitist Foundation and their stupid rules. Promoting based on skill, my ass!" this section breaks the writing style of an addendum fairly quickly. I might believe it later in the article, but it's much too jarring here. It feels more like a personal journal than an addendum, and the formatting does not reflect this.

5. The actual journal section text is very, very small and difficult to read. Use ++ or a different format for legibility.

6. Overall, grammar, tone, and pacing could use a few more passes, but otherwise there weren't many major hiccups.

It's a good story, and I like the repetitious nature of the scip. I think you need to employ more subtlety in the early portions of the article, and let the full effects shine through better in the closing addendum.

item.title: SCP-4765
item.content:encoded:
Hello to everybody reading this!
This is my first SCP, many thanks to Mew-ltiverse and LiterallyMechanical for the greenlights and AKFrost and a few others I do not remember for crit!

Edit: based on feedback from T Rutherford and AtlasIncarnate, I have slightly altered the ordering of the skip and the incident in it to help with pacing and subtlety
